raw_consumer_statement: I had multiple charges on my credit card by a local merchant for services that were never rendered. I disputed with American Express and they started their investigation and reversed all the charges to my card but said if any further info was provided XXXX XX/XX/year> which was about 30 days out. The merchant was not disputing the reversal of any of these charges that totaled about XXXX. I thought this matter was behind me but to my utter dismay Amex reversed the XXXX and said they determined it was my responsibility on XX/XX/year>. Their reasoning was that it was determined that I'm either family member, employee, business partner, authorized signer to the merchant. Not one of these items are the case and I actually had the merchant sign and notarize an affidavit also confirming that none of these apply and that all these charges should be reversed and credit my account. I forwarded this document from the merchant to Amex and they said that their decision is still the same. Furthermore, this merchant has charged many other credit cards of consumers which included Amex customers which was reversed. Not sure how any of this is legal and when asking Amex what info did they obtain to make their decision?? They stated they do not need to provide that info to me on what info they are referencing. This is utterly ridiculous!!
